description: Complete the Bun-to-Node migration (phase 2). Phase 1 is already on main: tsx installed, bun-types removed, isBun removed, build scripts migrated, package.json scripts use npm, proxy dev server built. This phase converts E2E infrastructure, CI/Docker, and docs.

userStories.title: Convert e2e/test-server.ts from Bun.serve() to node:http
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want the main E2E test server to use Node.js APIs so Bun is not needed for E2E tests.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/test-server.ts uses node:http createServer instead of Bun.serve()
All request handling logic preserved (same routes, same responses, same headers)
The standalone server mode still works: npx tsx e2e/test-server.ts
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
userStories.notes: ~320-line file. Bun.serve() uses a fetch handler that receives Request and returns Response (Web API). Node http.createServer uses (req, res) callbacks. Patterns: request.url->req.url, request.method->req.method, request.json()->collect body+JSON.parse, new Response(body,{status,headers})->res.writeHead(status,headers)+res.end(body), Response.json(data)->res.writeHead(200,{'content-type':'application/json'})+res.end(JSON.stringify(data)). The file exports startServer returning {server,port,baseUrl}. server.stop()->server.close().

userStories.title: Convert e2e/strict-csp-test-server.ts from Bun.serve() to node:http
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want the CSP test server to use Node.js APIs.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/strict-csp-test-server.ts uses node:http createServer instead of Bun.serve()
All CSP headers and response logic preserved
npm run type-check passes
userStories.notes: ~135-line file. Same conversion pattern as US-001. Preserve all Content-Security-Policy headers.

userStories.title: Convert e2e/analyze-site-test-server.ts from Bun.serve() to node:http
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want the analyze-site test server to use Node.js APIs.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/analyze-site-test-server.ts uses node:http createServer instead of Bun.serve()
All routes and response logic preserved
npm run type-check passes
userStories.notes: ~980-line file, most complex test server. Many routes serving different HTML pages. Same conversion pattern as US-001 but larger. Take care with body parsing for POST routes.

userStories.title: Convert e2e/fixtures.ts MCP server spawning from bun to node, run full E2E suite
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want E2E tests to spawn the MCP server using Node instead of Bun.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/fixtures.ts spawns 'node' instead of 'bun' for the MCP server
Hot reload uses the dev proxy or node --watch instead of bun --hot
e2e/build-icons.e2e.ts uses 'npx tsx' instead of 'bun'
e2e/global-setup.ts error messages reference npm instead of bun
All bun comments in e2e/*.ts files updated
e2e/CLAUDE.md updated
npm run test:e2e passes (all 357 E2E tests)
userStories.notes: Critical story. fixtures.ts spawns MCP server via spawn('bun',['--hot',...]). Change to spawn the dev proxy (platform/mcp-server/src/dev-proxy.ts built in phase 1) or node --watch. Hot reload tests (lifecycle.e2e.ts) test connections survive code changes — needs proxy. The wrapper file trick (append comment to trigger bun --hot) needs replacement with proxy's change detection (touch dist/ or send signal). Check dev-proxy.ts to understand its interface.

userStories.title: Migrate CI workflow from Bun to Node/npm
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want CI to use Node.js and npm.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml uses actions/setup-node@v4 instead of oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
CI uses npm ci instead of bun install --frozen-lockfile
CI uses npm run instead of bun run
CI cache key uses package-lock.json instead of bun.lock
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
userStories.notes: 7 CI jobs, each with setup-bun + bun install + bun run. Replace all. Cache key hashes bun.lock — change to package-lock.json. E2E job: bunx playwright install -> npx playwright install.

userStories.title: Migrate Dockerfile and Ralph scripts, delete bun.lock files, generate package-lock.json
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want Docker and Ralph infrastructure to use Node/npm.
acceptanceCriteria: .ralph/Dockerfile removes Bun install section
.ralph/ralph.sh uses npm ci and npm run
.ralph/worker.sh uses npm run for quality checks
.ralph/perfect-docs.sh qualityChecks use npm run
.ralph/RALPH.md references npm
.claude/skills/ralph/__SKILL__.md references npm in quality checks
All bun.lock files deleted (root, plugins/e2e-test, plugins/slack, docs)
package-lock.json generated via npm install
npm run build passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes: Dockerfile base image (node:24) already has Node/npm. Remove BUN_INSTALL env and curl bun.sh lines. ralph.sh setup: bun install->npm ci, bun run build->npm run build. worker.sh: all bun run->npm run. Delete bun.lock everywhere. Run npm install at root to generate package-lock.json. Do NOT touch consumer.sh in opentabs-prds (separate repo).

userStories.title: Update all documentation to reference Node.js/npm
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want all docs to match the Node.js/npm toolchain.
acceptanceCriteria: CLAUDE.md: all commands use npm, tech stack says Node.js, isBun docs removed, tests says vitest
README.md: prereqs say Node.js 22+, all commands use npm
CONTRIBUTING.md: all commands use npm, Bun-first APIs removed
docs site MDX files updated for npm
platform/mcp-server/CLAUDE.md: proxy dev server instead of bun --hot
platform/cli/CLAUDE.md: updated for npm
docs/components/illustrations.tsx: bun refs updated
Zero bun references in .md/.mdx files (verify with grep)
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: Documentation-only. Use grep -r 'bun run\|bun install\|bun --hot\|bun:test\|Bun\.' --include='*.md' --include='*.mdx' to find all refs and verify when done.
